data.title: The Great Standstill: American Workers Stay Put As Job Switching Appetite Hits Five-Year Low Amid Uncertainty
data.description: According to the latest SCE Labor Market Survey from the New York Federal Reserve, job satisfaction in the United States has reached an all-time low. Data reveals that only 9.7% of workers are considering switching employers, the lowest figure in five years. Furthermore, satisfaction levels regarding wage compensation and promotion opportunities have hit record lows since the survey's inception in 2014. The survey also indicates a decrease in the number of individuals actively seeking new employment, dropping from 23.8% in November 2025 to 22.5%. This decline is particularly notable among workers under 45 and female employees. Job mobility has primarily been hindered by workers without a college degree. Interestingly, despite the decrease in job switching, the average reservation wage has reached a peak of $84,762 in March. For further details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: SEGG Media Enters Sports Predictions Market via Sports.com Predict
data.description: Fort Worth, Texas-based Sports Entertainment Gaming Global Corporation (NASDAQ: SEGG, LTRYW) revealed the introduction of Sports.com Predict on April 24, 2026. This move signifies the Company's venture into the expanding arena of prediction markets. The project will be managed by Sports Predicts Limited, a subsidiary of SEGG Media, and will be seamlessly integrated into the existing Sports.com platform.

data.title: These Analysts Raise Their Forecasts On VeriSign After Strong Q1 Results
data.description: VeriSign, Inc (NASDAQ: VRSN) reported strong first-quarter earnings on Thursday, surpassing analyst expectations. The company posted earnings of $2.34 per share, exceeding the consensus estimate of $2.25 per share. Quarterly sales also surpassed expectations, reaching $428.900 million compared to the anticipated $425.912 million. VeriSign has increased its sales guidance for FY2026 from the initial range of $1.715 billion-$1.735 billion to a new projection of $1.730 billion-$1.745 billion. The company emphasized its commitment to maintaining 100% availability of its resolution service for the.com/.net domains, now in its 29th year. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Union Pacific Analysts Increase Their Forecasts Following Upbeat Q1 Earnings
data.description: Union Pacific Corp (NYSE: UNP) announced earnings for the first quarter that surpassed expectations. The company reported first-quarter 2026 diluted EPS of $2.87 and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.93, exceeding analyst estimates of $2.86. Operating revenue grew by 3% year over year to $6.217 billion, surpassing estimates of $6.199 billion. CEO Jim Vena mentioned, "Our safety, service, and operating momentum continued in the first quarter as we further challenged ‘what's possible' from our great railroad." The company confirmed its 2026 outlook, projecting mid-single-digit EPS growth, improved operating ratio, and robust cash generation. Union Pacific stock price declined by 0.2% to $270.85 on Friday. Following the earnings report, analysts adjusted their price targets for Union Pacific.

data.title: Oilfield Giant Baker Hughes Defies Middle East Chaos With Stellar Earnings Beat
data.description: Shares of Baker Hughes Co. (NASDAQ: BKR) surged around 4% following the release of their first-quarter results which exceeded expectations. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of 58 cents, surpassing the anticipated 49 cents. Quarterly sales stood at $6.587 billion, higher than the expected $6.335 billion. Revenues saw a 2% increase compared to the previous year, with net income skyrocketing by 131% to $930 million. Baker Hughes highlighted its strategic portfolio management plan, notably including the divestiture of Waygate Technologies, projected to bring in around $3 billion in gross proceeds for the company this year. Despite significant disruptions in the market, the company's performance was strong.

data.title: American Express Reports Q1 Beat, Stock Tanks On 2026 Outlook
data.description: American Express Co (NYSE: AXP) saw a sharp decline in its shares during early trading on Friday following the release of its first-quarter results. Key insights from analysts include BofA Securities' Mihir Bhatia maintaining a Buy rating and increasing the price target from $381 to $387, while BTIG analyst Vincent Caintic held a Sell rating with a price target of $285. BofA Securities noted that American Express exceeded expectations with revenue reaching $18.907 billion and earnings per share hitting $4.28, surpassing Street estimates of $18.642 billion and $4.06 per share. Bhatia attributed the beat to improved revenue and credit provisions, despite higher OpEx. For further details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: AWS Marketplace now offers Chainlink Crypto Oracle Services for cloud-to-blockchain connectivity, boosting enterprise efficiency.
data.description: Amazon has recently implemented Chainlink integration, enabling enterprises to link cloud infrastructure with blockchain networks using well-known AWS tools. This move allows for seamless connectivity and collaboration between the two platforms, enhancing efficiency and convenience. The integration capitalizes on Amazon's extensive reach and Chainlink's advanced technology, benefiting a wide range of businesses. This innovative approach is set to revolutionize the way enterprises interact with blockchain networks, potentially leading to increased adoption and utilization.

data.title: FEDERAL HOME LOAN BANK OF BOSTON ANNOUNCES 2026 FIRST QUARTER RESULTS, DECLARES DIVIDEND
data.description: In Boston on April 24, 2026, the Federal Home Loan Bank of Boston disclosed its initial unaudited financial outcomes for the first quarter of 2026, revealing a net profit of $44.3 million. The bank anticipates submitting its Form 10-Q quarterly report for the mentioned period soon.
